rationale: A anchors the right Flink 2.2.0 docs, the exact `JobResourceRequirementsUpdateHandler`, and feature-adjacent tests for resource-requirements. B drifts to generic job-handler tests and adds unsupported “retrieval system” claims. A is still slightly uncertain about the exact DTO/test names, but it is closer to the relevant API surface and safer for implementation work.

A: It hedges on the DTO/test names instead of confirming them from source.
It may overstate the existence of `JobResourceRequirementsUpdateHandlerTest.java` without verification.
B: It replaces feature-specific neighboring tests with generic job handler tests that are not the right anchor.
It introduces unsupported meta-claims about retrieval rather than source-backed evidence.
Its cited docs fragments (`part-0032`, `part-0002`) are less directly useful than naming the actual endpoint section and related feature tests.
